_meta.title: SEALED — One Afternoon: The Embassy, The Date, The Half You Weren't Shown (long-form v2)
_meta.purpose: v2 replacement of live SSuO2KOXr0Y. Explainer-first: Remotion slates/maps/timelines/source-cards + image-kenburns Wikipedia photos for real figures (NO kling/hedra synthetic faces, NO Sora hyperreal). Hold-both-tell-them-nothing tone. '60' numeral on screen ONLY on the gated OCHA-55/COI-73 source card (Beat 5). 3rd-grade voter script.
_meta.death_toll_lock: Spoken/thumbnail figure = 60. On-screen numeral '60' must co-render with OCHA-55 / COI-73 footnote (clip eb5-01). Hook frames carry NO numeral.
_meta.comment_trigger: If the promise was kept, what do you call the same afternoon?

props.text: May 14, 2018. A promise was kept. You were shown one half.

expected_on_screen_text: May 14, 2018. A promise was kept. You were shown one half.
clips.covers_script_section: Beat 1 — hook: 'On May fourteenth, 2018, a promise was kept... you were only shown one half.' (NO '60' numeral on hook frame — founder lock)
NEW OPEN (gravitas) — 'Some things get written down on purpose.'

props.text: A date. A count. A signed paper — so they cannot be quietly erased.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW OPEN (gravitas) — 'A date. A count. A signed paper. They are written down so they cannot be quietly erased.' [pause:400ms]
NEW OPEN (gravitas) — 'That is what a record is for. It is how power is kept from showing you only the part it likes.'

props.text: You remember the day the embassy moved. Maybe you picked a side. That's fair.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 1 — empathy acknowledgment: 'Maybe you picked a side that afternoon. That's fair.' [pause:400ms]

props.text: TWO THINGS HAPPENED. YOU REMEMBER ONE.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 1 — open loop: 'One spring afternoon. Two things happened... You only remember half of it.'
Beat 2 — 'For twenty years, four presidents said one thing: not yet.'

events.label: Law lets any president pause the move

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 2 — '1995 law... He just signed a paper every six months. Clinton signed it. Bush signed it. Obama signed it.'
Beat 2 — 'Then one president stopped. And what they declined, somebody signed.' (Wikipedia photo, slow push-in)
Beat 3 — 'December sixth, two thousand seventeen. The first half landed on the record.'

props.quote: The United States recognizes Jerusalem as the capital of Israel.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 3 — 'The United States said Jerusalem was the capital. It was signed. It was dated. It was real. But a signature is not a building.'
Beat 4 — 'May fourteenth, 2018. The ribbon was cut. The embassy opened in Jerusalem.'
Beat 4 — embassy move map (no faces)
Beat 4 — 'This was a top A-I-P-A-C priority.' (no numeric rank — founder lock)

props.quote: A pro-Israel lobby in Washington that works to shape U.S. policy.
props.source: AIPAC — American Israel Public Affairs Committee

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 4 — 'In other words, a group in Washington... a pro-Israel lobby. It works to shape U.S. policy. One date. One ribbon. But the date carried more.'

props.quote: UN OCHA counted 55 killed that day. A later UN inquiry counted 73 who died of that day's wounds. 60 was the toll reported as the count rose.
props.source: UN OCHA, May 14, 2018 · UN Commission of Inquiry, 2019

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 5 — [[FOOTNOTE SLOT]] GATED: 'The same afternoon, sixty people died at the Gaza border.' The ONLY frame where the numeral '60' is allowed on screen — co-renders with OCHA-55 / COI-73 verbatim locked text. Founder death-toll lock.

props.text: These were real people. They died on a real day.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 5 — empathy/why-this-matters human breath: 'These were real people. They died on a real day.' [pause:300ms] (sequenced BEFORE the clinical frame returns per Stage 12.5)

props.text: We are not here to tell you what to feel. We are only here to show you the date.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 5 — 'We are not here to tell you what to feel. We are only here to show you the date. The number is the receipt.'

props.text: SAME AFTERNOON. ONE DATE HELD BOTH.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 5 — 'One afternoon held both. A ribbon in Jerusalem. A count at the border. You saw one half for seven years. The other half was on the front page.'
Beat 6 — 'Now, was anyone keeping a tally? Yes. And it came earlier than you think.'

props.title: UN VOTE ON THE NEW CAPITAL

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 6 — 'In December, the UN voted on the new capital. The count was one hundred twenty-eight to nine.'

props.quote: 128 to 9 — condemning the December recognition. Five months before the May afternoon.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 6 — 'That vote was about the December decision. It was not about this May afternoon. But the number is public.' (UN vote kept on its OWN date, Dec 21 2017 — founder lock)

events.label: A vote already on the books

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 6 — 'So the date had a ribbon. It had a toll. And a vote was already on the books.'

props.promise: Move the U.S. embassy to Jerusalem

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 7 — 'The SEALED book grades this promise number seventy-four. KEPT. That grade is narrow. It grades the move itself. It does not grade the afternoon.'

props.text: A promise can be kept. And it can still share a date with the rest of the story.

clips.covers_script_section: Beat 7 — cold-close warmth: 'Here is what this afternoon taught us... Both are documented. Both share one date.'
NEW CLOSE (gravitas) — 'Both halves were written down. That is the part that matters.' [pause:400ms]

props.text: A date on a page does not take sides. It keeps both halves where you can find them.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW CLOSE (gravitas) — 'A date on a page does not take sides. It just keeps both halves where you can find them.'

props.text: The record gives you the whole afternoon — not the half someone picked for you.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW CLOSE (gravitas) — 'The record is why you get the whole afternoon, and not the half someone picked for you.'
Beat 7 — 'You are allowed to hold both. Nobody tells you what to feel. You just deserve the whole afternoon.'

props.name: If the promise was kept, what do you call the same afternoon?
props.detail: Full receipt + free newsletter linked below · SEALED

expected_on_screen_text: If the promise was kept, what do you call the same afternoon?
Full receipt + free newsletter linked below · SEALED
clips.covers_script_section: Beat 7 — end card: 'The full receipt and the free newsletter are linked below.' [pause:1.2s] + pinned comment trigger

music_cues.mood: music recedes to near-silence under the toll card · no swell
